criteria.priority_notes: Excellence evaluation for this topic should focus on: (a) quality and scientific ambition of the proposed approach to advancing AI agent autonomy, planning mechanisms, memory management, and reasoning capabilities; (b) methodological soundness of multi-agent collaboration framework design including mixed AI architectures and multimodal reasoning; (c) quality and rigour of proposed KPIs, benchmarking approaches, and progress monitoring mechanisms, which are explicitly required in the scope text. No explicit weighting is stated in the topic text or call extract.
criteria.assumption_note: The evaluation criterion label 'Excellence' is not explicitly stated in the topic text or call extract. This is the standard first evaluation criterion for RIA under Horizon Europe. The priority notes are derived from scope text emphasis on specific technical quality dimensions. Explicit criterion weights are not stated in any available source document. Criterion label and weight must be verified against the applicable Tier 2A evaluation form before downstream use.

criteria.priority_notes: Impact evaluation for this topic should emphasise: (a) contribution to the Apply AI Strategy, which is explicitly stated as a required output; (b) applicability across the target sectors (healthcare, advanced manufacturing, in-vehicle autonomous driving) identified in the portfolio coverage award procedure; (c) deployment pathway through EDIHs and the AI-on-demand platform; (d) contribution to ADRA partnership objectives and synergies with international initiatives. No explicit weighting is stated in the topic text or call extract.
criteria.assumption_note: The evaluation criterion label 'Impact' is not explicitly stated in the topic text or call extract. This is the standard second evaluation criterion for RIA under Horizon Europe. The priority notes are derived from stated requirements and deployment context in the source documents. Explicit criterion weights are not stated in any available source document. Criterion label and weight must be verified against the applicable Tier 2A evaluation form before downstream use.

criteria.priority_notes: Implementation evaluation for this topic should consider: (a) consortium composition and adequacy given the co-programmed ADRA partnership nature of the topic; (b) feasibility and credibility of planned linkages to AI Factories, Data Labs, and Testing and Experiment Facilities; (c) workplan quality including allocation of cohesion activity tasks for ADRA and the GenAI4EU central Hub, which are explicitly mandatory. No explicit weighting is stated in the topic text or call extract.
criteria.assumption_note: The evaluation criterion label 'Implementation' is not explicitly stated in the topic text or call extract. This is the standard third evaluation criterion for RIA under Horizon Europe. The priority notes are derived from mandatory requirements stated in the source documents. Explicit criterion weights are not stated in any available source document. Criterion label and weight must be verified against the applicable Tier 2A evaluation form before downstream use.

criteria.priority_notes: At the award stage, grants are awarded to ensure at least one funded project per sector — healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving — subject to passing all evaluation thresholds. This procedure exception may override strict rank ordering at the award stage. Proposals targeting one of these three sectors gain eligibility for the sector-slot selection mechanism.
